claim: Polylogue can run and inspect a bounded prose-only embedding backfill on the active archive, expose material catch-up progress separately from zero-progress daemon scans, and keep semantic-search coverage visible through status/preflight artifacts.
non_claim: This is not full embedding convergence; thousands of eligible sessions remain pending, and the active prod daemon may still write older zero-count catch-up rows until deployed code catches up.
caveats: The bounded run used --max-sessions 25 --max-messages 5000 --max-cost-usd 1 and completed after 25 candidate sessions.
Preflight cost is approximate; actual charged/provider cost is not independently reconciled here.
The status latest_catchup_run can still show a newer zero-progress daemon scan until deployed code catches up; latest_material_catchup_run plus before/after status are the proof for this packet.
